text: When designing a REST API, following best practices ensures it is reliable and easy to use. Start by creating clear and logical endpoints, such as `/users` or `/products`, so developers understand the API's functionality. Always use appropriate HTTP status codes, like 200 for success or 404 for not found, to communicate results effectively. Good documentation is essential; it should explain how to use each endpoint, with examples. Finally, implement versioning, for example, `/v1/users`, to avoid breaking changes for existing users when you update the API. This approach makes your API maintainable and developer-friendly.
